title: N003: Add optional Webhook and monitoring templates
summary: Implementation adds 7 new conditionally-rendered Helm templates (webhook Service, MutatingWebhookConfiguration, ValidatingWebhookConfiguration, cert-manager Issuer, cert-manager Certificate, ServiceMonitor) and updates 3 existing templates (Deployment, NetworkPolicy, NOTES.txt) with conditional webhook support. All 296 helm-unittest tests pass across 17 test suites. Cross-references between templates (webhook service name in clientConfig, Certificate dnsNames, cert-manager annotation, Deployment secret volume) are internally consistent and verified correct under fullnameOverride. All features properly gated by values flags. Cert-manager templates use an improved dual guard (webhook.enabled AND certmanager.enabled) preventing orphaned resources.

tests_checklist.item: Enabled/disabled toggle tested for all features (webhook, certmanager, serviceMonitor, networkPolicy)
Edge case: webhook enabled + certmanager disabled tested
fullnameOverride propagation tested for all new resources
Cross-references tested (webhook service in clientConfig, cert annotation, issuerRef, dnsNames)
Integration tests verify total document counts and feature suppression

code_quality_checklist.item: All templates follow existing patterns (conditional guard, standard labels, fullname helper, Release.Namespace)
webhookServiceName helper centralizes webhook service name across templates
No dead code or commented-out code
Template indentation and YAML structure correct
values.yaml has clear documentation comments for all new values
servingCertName helper missing per task 3.2 (cert name inlined in 3 templates)

security_checklist.item: Webhook failurePolicy set to Fail (not Ignore)
NetworkPolicy restricts ingress to required ports only
ServiceMonitor uses bearerToken auth and HTTPS scheme

architecture_checklist.item: Each optional feature independently toggleable via values
Cert-manager guard improved with dual condition (webhook.enabled AND certmanager.enabled)
Cross-references use helper functions for consistency
Template structure mirrors Kustomize source accurately
NOTES.txt warns when webhook enabled without cert-manager

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No unnecessary features beyond plan scope
Cert name pattern centralized via helper

fail_fast_checklist.item: Webhook failurePolicy: Fail ensures invalid CRs rejected
Conditional guards prevent rendering invalid partial configurations

defensive_checklist.item: DNS name truncation at 63 chars in helpers
trimSuffix prevents trailing dashes after truncation

issues_found.description: Task 3.2 specified adding a `memcached-operator.servingCertName` helper to centralize the cert name pattern. Only `webhookServiceName` was added. The cert name `{{ include "memcached-operator.fullname" . }}-webhook-cert` is repeated inline in certificate.yaml:5, mutatingwebhookconfiguration.yaml:10, and validatingwebhookconfiguration.yaml:10. The secret name `-webhook-cert-secret` is repeated in certificate.yaml:18 and deployment.yaml:87.
issues_found.fix: Add a `memcached-operator.webhookCertName` helper returning `{{ printf "%s-webhook-cert" (include "memcached-operator.fullname" .) | trunc 63 | trimSuffix "-" }}` and use it in all 5 locations. This centralizes the name and prevents drift if the naming convention changes.

issues_found.description: ServiceMonitor uses `port: metrics` instead of `port: https` specified in the source config/prometheus/monitor.yaml and plan task 1.6. This is actually CORRECT because the Deployment template names port 8443 as `metrics` (deployment.yaml:55), so the port reference must match. The plan text was inaccurate relative to the existing Deployment template naming.
issues_found.fix: No change needed. The implementation correctly adapted the port name to match the actual Deployment port name.

suggested_improvements: Add `memcached-operator.webhookCertName` helper to _helpers.tpl (per task 3.2) to centralize the `-webhook-cert` suffix used in 5 locations across 3 templates
Consider adding `memcached-operator.webhookCertSecretName` helper for the `-webhook-cert-secret` suffix used in certificate.yaml and deployment.yaml
